name: Community Bank Of Santa Maria
description_short: Community Bank of Santa Maria is an FDIC-insured community bank founded by former Bank of Santa Maria employees to provide local decision-making and personalized service to the Santa Maria Valley.
description_long:
Community Bank of Santa Maria was founded by Jim Glines and Bill Hares, two career bankers who previously worked at Bank of Santa Maria. When that bank was acquired by a larger regional institution, they recognized that community needs were no longer being adequately met and decided to establish a new bank with a explicit focus on local decision-making and community service. The bank was built around recruiting board members and staff who are Santa Maria Valley residents first, ensuring leadership that understands and lives within the communities they serve.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of financial services including personal and business checking and savings accounts, personal loans, business loans, and specialized agricultural lending products marketed as "Ag Loans." They provide digital banking services such as online and mobile banking, a digital wallet with tap-to-pay functionality, rapid remote deposit, telephone banking, and business cash management tools. Additional offerings include debit cards with card management features, fraud prevention services, and account switching assistance through their ClickSWITCH tool. The bank maintains multiple physical branch locations with a main branch accessible at (805) 922-2900.

What distinguishes Community Bank of Santa Maria is their explicit emphasis on local ownership and decision-making—a direct response to the consolidation trend that prompted the founders to establish the bank. Board members and staff are required to be community residents who participate in local schools, churches, and little league activities, creating accountability to the communities they serve. This localized governance model contrasts with regional and national banks where decision-making is centralized. The bank also emphasizes agricultural lending, suggesting particular expertise serving the Santa Maria Valley's farming community.

As a full-service community bank, Community Bank of Santa Maria serves customers seeking traditional banking with local oversight and relationship-based service. The main caveat is that as a smaller institution, they may have more limited product options and technological capabilities compared to larger banks, though their website demonstrates modern digital banking features. Their focus on the Santa Maria Valley means this bank is primarily positioned for local residents and businesses rather than serving a national market. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Debit cards with card suite management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to $250,000
Local decision-making and board governance comprised entirely of Santa Maria Valley residents and community leaders
Specialized agricultural lending products ("Ag Loans") serving the local farming industry
Modern digital banking including mobile wallet with tap-to-pay, remote deposit, and online banking
Staff and board members are community residents involved in local schools, churches, and civic activities
Explicit community focus and relationship-based banking model as alternative to regional consolidation
Business cash management services and commercial/residential construction lending available
cons: Limited to Santa Maria Valley market, requiring customers to be local or willing to travel for in-person services
As a smaller community bank, likely offers fewer products and investment options than large regional/national banks
Website does not specify interest rates, fees, or competitive pricing information for any products
No mention of insurance products, investment services, or wealth management beyond basic lending and deposits
Single main branch location referenced on website, potentially limiting accessibility compared to larger bank networks

contact.address: 2739 Santa Maria Way, Santa Maria, CA 93455

best_for: Santa Maria Valley small business owners seeking relationship-based lending and local decision-making
Local agricultural and farming businesses needing specialized ag loans with community-focused service
Community members prioritizing banking with local oversight and accountability over large regional institutions
Customers seeking FDIC-insured deposits and basic banking services from a locally-owned financial institution
diagnosis: Community Bank of Santa Maria is best for Santa Maria Valley residents and businesses seeking FDIC-insured banking with local decision-making and relationship-based service, particularly agricultural businesses. The main caveat is that this is a geographically limited community bank serving primarily local markets, so it is not suitable for customers requiring national branch networks or advanced investment services.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 2739 Santa Maria Way, Santa Maria, CA 93455
meta_description: Community Bank of Santa Maria is an FDIC-insured community bank providing local decision-making and personalized service in Santa Maria Valley.
